SOURCE: www.cbsnews.com

Trump, Zelenskyy signal progress in Ukraine peace talks after Mar-a-Lago meeting

President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met Sunday at Mar-a-Lago to work towards a peace plan between Russia and Ukraine. HEADLINE: Russia-Ukraine war: List of key events, day 1,404
SOURCE: www.aljazeera.com
PUBLISHED: 2025-12-29 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2025-12-29 00:00:00
URL: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/29/russia-ukraine-war-list-of-key-events-day-1404

Russia-Ukraine war: List of key events, day 1,404

These are the key developments from day 1,404 of Russia’s war on Ukraine. Share
Here is where things stand on Monday, December 29:
Diplomacy
United States President Donald Trump hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for talks at his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida and said the two leaders were “getting a lot closer, maybe very close” to a deal to end the war in Ukraine. Trump and Zelenskyy reported progress on two of the most contentious issues in the peace talks: security guarantees for Ukraine and the division of eastern Ukraine’s Donbas region that Russia has sought to capture. On security guarantees, Zelenskyy said that a deal had been reached, while Trump said they were 95 percent of the way to such an agreement. Both Trump and Zelenskyy said that the future of the mostly Russian-occupied Donbas had not been settled, though the US president said discussions were “moving in the right direction”. “It’s unresolved, but it’s getting a lot closer. That’s a very tough issue,” Trump said. The two leaders did not offer further details or a deadline for completing the deal, but Zelenskyy said any peace agreement would have to be approved by Ukraine’s parliament or by a referendum. Shortly after the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting, Russian President Vladimir Putin’s special envoy, Kirill Dmitriev, wrote on X that the “whole world appreciates” Trump and his team’s peace efforts. Ahead of the meeting with Zelenskyy, Trump and Putin spoke for more than two hours on the telephone. The US president described the call as “excellent” and “productive”. Trump said he would call Putin again after the meeting with Zelenskyy. Kremlin foreign policy aide Yury Ushakov said the initial call was “friendly” and that Putin had told Trump that a 60-day ceasefire, proposed by the European Union and Ukraine, would simply prolong the war. Ushakov said that a “bold, responsible, political decision is needed from Kyiv” on the Donbas region and other disputed matters for there to be a “complete cessation” of hostilities.

Bangladesh’s Gen Z party faces revolt over Islamist alliance, risking its future

DHAKA – A Bangladeshi youth-driven party born out of the country’s 2024 uprising is facing an open revolt from within after sealing an
election alliance with an Islamist group
, a move analysts say could jeopardise its future and reinforce established parties. At least 30 senior leaders of the National Citizen Party (NCP) have openly opposed its alliance with Jamaat-e-Islami, announced on Dec 28 , with several resigning in protest. The Muslim-majority South Asian nation goes to the polls on Feb 12. Before the deal, opinion polls had predicted Jamaat finishing a close second behind the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) of former prime minister Khaleda Zia, while the NCP lagged far behind in third place. “The NCP presented itself as a youth-driven alternative to traditional power structures. That identity is now under serious strain,” said Mr H.M. Nazmul Alam, an academic. “Youth-based movements do not collapse only because they lose elections. They collapse when they lose clarity and internal unity,” he said. ‘Alliance for greater unity’
The NCP was formed earlier in 2025 by leaders of the protests that ousted long-time prime minister Sheikh Hasina in August 2024, forcing her to flee to India. With the Awami League banned, the vote will be effectively a direct contest between the BNP and Jamaat, which has trailed the other two in the past and had not been allowed to contest any elections since 2013 after a court said its registration as a political party conflicted with Bangladesh’s secular Constitution. An interim government led by Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus revoked all the restrictions on Jamaat in August 2024.

has forged an electoral alliance with Islamist party Jamaat-e-Islami ahead of February’s parliamentary election, stirring internal rifts. NCP chief Nahid Islam said on Dec 28 that the party had decided to enter into the alliance for greater unity, adding that the final list of candidates would be announced on Dec 29. Critics of the NCP’s move said it undermined the party’s founding ideals. “The moral support I had for the NCP will no longer exist due to this ideological mismatch,” said one university student, asking not to be named. The partnership comes amid broader political realignments, with the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) – aligned with ailing former prime minister Khaleda Zia and effectively led by her son, acting chair Tarique Rahman – regaining momentum following Mr Rahman’s return after nearly 17 years in exile.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,re
p='articles/2025/12/29/articles.jsonl'
terms=['Board of Peace','Palestinian technocrats','Jamaat-e-Islami','National Citizen Party','Brock Purdy','Philadelphia Eagles','New England Patriots','Ruben Amorim','Brahim Diaz','Mar-a-Lago','Trump, Zelenskyy','Zelenskyy signal progress']
seen=set(); out=0
with open(p) as f:
    for line in f:
        a=json.loads(line)
        text=((a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or ''))
        hit=None
        for t in terms:
            if t.lower() in text.lower():
                hit=t; break
        if not hit: continue
        key=(a.get('source'),a.get('date_publish'),a.get('title'))
        if key in seen: continue
        seen.add(key)
        print('TERM:',hit)
        print('SRC/DATE:',a.get('source'),a.get('date_publish'))
        print('TITLE:',a.get('title'))
        idx=text.lower().find(hit.lower())
        start=max(0,idx-250); end=min(len(text),idx+500)
        snippet=re.sub(r'\\s+',' ',text[start:end])
        print('SNIP:',snippet)
        print()
        out+=1
        if out>=50: break
print('unique printed', out)
PY"

Bangladesh’s Gen Z party faces revolt over Islamist alliance, risking its future

DHAKA – A Bangladeshi youth-driven party born out of the country’s 2024 uprising is facing an open revolt from within after sealing an
election alliance with an Islamist group
, a move analysts say could jeopardise its future and reinforce established parties. At least 30 senior leaders of the National Citizen Party (NCP) have openly opposed its alliance with Jamaat-e-Islami, announced on Dec 28 , with several resigning in protest. The Muslim-majority South Asian nation goes to the polls on Feb 12. Before the deal, opinion polls had predicted Jamaat finishing a close second behind the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) of former prime minister Khaleda Zia, while the NCP lagged far behind in third place. “The NCP presented itself as a youth-driven alternative to traditional power structures. That identity is now under serious strain,” said Mr H.M. Nazmul Alam, an academic. “Youth-based movements do not collapse only because they lose elections. They collapse when they lose clarity and internal unity,” he said. ‘Alliance for greater unity’
The NCP was formed earlier in 2025 by leaders of the protests that ousted long-time prime minister Sheikh Hasina in August 2024, forcing her to flee to India. Driven by Gen Z activists born after the late 1990s, it says it aims to free the nation from decades of nepotism and the dominance of Hasina’s Awami League and the BNP. With the Awami League banned, the vote will be effectively a direct contest between the BNP and Jamaat, which has trailed the other two in the past and had not been allowed to contest any elections since 2013 after a court said its registration as a political party conflicted with Bangladesh’s secular Constitution. An interim government led by Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus revoked all the restrictions on Jamaat in August 2024.

Bangladesh is moving toward a national election on 12 February 2026. Political parties are watching closely for signs that election rules are being applied fairly, and some could threaten legal or political action if they believe the process is biased. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Public statements by party leaders or spokespersons, party press briefings, and corroborating reports from major news organizations.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves upon the first clear public statement, made no later than January 20, 2026, that a party may reconsider its participation in the February 12, 2026 election because of the dispute over candidate eligibility and foreign citizenship.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The official English name of the party making that statement. If multiple parties clearly qualify, resolve to the party whose qualifying statement is first documented in reliable public reporting.
### 304287 Which party will emerge as the main opposition in Bangladesh's new parliament after the 2026 election, based on seat results reported by February 13, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-14. Bangladesh is preparing for a parliamentary election. In a parliament, the main opposition is generally the largest party outside the governing side. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: The question will resolve using Bangladesh Election Commission seat results made public on or before February 13, 2026, together with consistent reporting by Reuters or major Bangladeshi newspapers identifying the party serving as the main opposition.
  • Resolution Date: The resolution occurs on the date by which the post-election seat standings and opposition status are clearly reported, no later than February 13, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the English name of the political party identified as the main opposition. A standard abbreviation is acceptable only if it uniquely identifies the same party.
### 806810 What will be the name of the Palestinian body set to take over Gaza's security and administration in the first phase of a proposed disarmament plan by April 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-12. Talks around Gaza's ceasefire, reconstruction, and future governance are ongoing. Diplomats and Palestinian actors are discussing who could run civilian administration and security if a phased plan moves forward. criteria:
  • The question resolves based on the officially circulated framework, public remarks from the Board of Peace or the United Nations Security Council, or consistent reporting from major international news organizations describing the plan.
  • Resolve by 30 April 2026, or earlier if the designated governing body is clearly identified in public documentation or authoritative reporting.
  • Provide the official English name of the body in 1-3 words, as stated in the source used for resolution.
### 857643 What peace body will Qatar's prime minister say Qatar has been invited to join in January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-30. Gulf states continue to play diplomatic roles in Middle East negotiations, and officials may announce participation in any new peace mechanism later this month. criteria:
  • Reuters' contemporaneous report on the Qatari prime minister's January 2026 remarks will be the primary source. If necessary, an official transcript, video, or government statement may be used to verify that the same body is being referenced, but Reuters' naming controls.
  • This question resolves when Reuters publicly reports the remarks, and no later than 31 January 2026.
  • Provide the exact name of the peace body in 1 to 3 words, matching the wording used in the controlling report.

Diplomats connected to the war between Russia and Ukraine are discussing possible face-to-face talks involving multiple governments in late January 2026. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official schedules or public statements from the governments involved, or, if those are unclear, consistent reporting from major international news organizations identifying the host city for the planned January 23-24, 2026 talks.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when the host city for those talks is publicly identified, and no later than 24 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: City name only, in common English usage.
### 655453 Which country will mediate the first face-to-face three-way talks among the US, Ukraine and Russia since the 2022 invasion, in January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-31. New diplomacy is under way around the war in Ukraine, including discussion of a direct meeting involving US, Ukrainian and Russian delegations. The mediator's country matters because it provides the diplomatic setting and framework for the talks. criteria:
  • Official communiques or readouts from the mediating government and from the US, Ukrainian or Russian delegations, corroborated by major news agencies such as Reuters or AP, will determine which country is acting as mediator.
  • The question resolves when the first such three-way talks are publicly confirmed in January 2026.
  • Provide the mediator country's full common English name, in 1-3 words.
### 575662 Where will the second round of US-brokered Russia-Ukraine peace talks begin on February 4, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-19. The United States is seeking to broker talks between Russia and Ukraine to try to end the war. A second round of trilateral negotiations is expected in early February 2026. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: official statements from the Ukrainian delegation, Russian delegation, US government representatives, or consistent reporting from at least two major international news organizations.
  • Resolution Date: 4 February 2026
  • Accepted Answer Format: the city name where the talks are reported to begin, written as a short location name.

  • Source of Truth: This question will resolve based on the official NFL final scoreboard, gamebook, or championship recap for Super Bowl LX. If multiple summaries differ, the official NFL gamebook and final score will control.
  • Resolution Date: The resolution occurs when Super Bowl LX is completed and officially declared final in February 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The full official team name of the Super Bowl LX winner must be provided exactly as listed by the NFL.
### 694686 Who will be the starting quarterback of the NFC team that reaches Super Bowl 2026 by January 26, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-17. The NFL playoffs are in the conference-championship stage. The quarterback officially starting for the NFC champion will be a key figure in that team’s path to Super Bowl 2026. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: The official NFL gamebook, box score, or game recap for the NFC championship game on NFL.com. Official team publications and major wire-service reports such as AP or Reuters may be used as secondary confirmation.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when the NFC championship game is final, expected no later than January 26, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The full name of the player officially listed as the starting quarterback for the NFC champion in that game.

  • Source of Truth: CAF’s official AFCON 2025 bracket and match schedule on CAFOnline.com. Major wire services such as Reuters or AP may be used for confirmation if needed.

  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the common English short country name of the team listed as Morocco’s semifinal opponent.
### 799398 Which Morocco player will take and miss the stoppage-time penalty in the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations final on 18 January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-14. The 2025 Africa Cup of Nations final is scheduled for 18 January 2026 between Morocco and Senegal. This question concerns any penalty awarded to Morocco during stoppage time of normal play, before extra time. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Resolve using the official CAF match report for the final. If the taker of the missed penalty is not clearly identified there, use contemporaneous Reuters or Associated Press match reports as secondary confirmation.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves when the match reports describing the penalty incident are publicly available, expected by 19 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the full name of the Moroccan player who takes that stoppage-time penalty and fails to score.
### 474377 Who will take charge of Manchester United in the Manchester derby against Manchester City on January 17, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-10. Manchester United are scheduled to play Manchester City in a Premier League derby in mid-January. Official club and league pre-match materials will identify who is leading United for that match. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official Manchester United match materials, the Premier League match centre, and widely cited post-match reports from major sports news outlets.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on January 17, 2026, when Manchester United officially list the person in charge for the derby, typically before kickoff.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the full name of the individual officially identified as the manager, head coach, or interim manager for Manchester United for that match.

blocks.content:
# 2025-12-24 Forecast Notes

Submitted initial forecasts for all 331 active questions.

Main evidence anchors used:
- Carabao Cup semi-final draw: Chelsea vs Arsenal/Crystal Palace winner; Newcastle vs Manchester City. Used Arsenal/Chelsea as most likely Man City final opponent.
- UEFA World Cup playoff draw: Italy path points to Wales/Bosnia; Kosovo path points to Turkey/Romania.
- Grammy nominations: Album of the Year field is GNX, Mayhem, Debi Tirar Mas Fotos, Man's Best Friend, etc.; Kendrick Lamar had the nomination lead.
- TikTok US JV agreement: Oracle, Silver Lake, MGX deal expected to close Jan. 22; CEO unknown, Shou Zi Chew retained as a conservative anchor.
- "Big armada" quote in current corpus explicitly points to South America.

Calibration:
- High-probability forecasts reserved for directly evidenced or structurally constrained items (e.g. Kerala for Nipah, Turkey for Grok ban, Board of Peace, Russia-linked Latvian drone, Taiwan arms sale).
- Most future incident/location/name questions were kept at 0.08-0.35 total mass due narrow answer spaces and weak evidence.
- Sports draw/opponent questions generally use multiple candidates with modest mass unless bracket evidence was known.

Watchlist for next updates:
- Near-Jan questions should be searched first: Chelsea coach, Ukraine reshuffle, Bangladesh T20 venue, AFCON Morocco/DR Congo, Greenland talks, Thailand NIDA poll, Australian Open draw/results.
- If new articles appear, update resolved-near-term questions aggressively; early forecasts are intentionally broad.

## 2025-12-25 Update

No resolution events in `../state.json`; all 331 predictions remained active.

Updates made:
- Morocco injury news: Romain Saiss has a current muscle injury; raised him for the Morocco calf-injury question while keeping Boufal/Mazraoui as backups.
- Bangladesh election reporting: Tarique Rahman/BNP look like government frontrunners; Jamaat-e-Islami described as the main rival after Awami League was blocked, so raised Jamaat for main opposition.
- Thailand reporting: Bhumjaithai named Sihasak as second PM nominee, but election still framed as Bhumjaithai vs People's Party; raised Natthaphong/Anutin and added Bhumjaithai to party-list poll.
- Yemen reporting named National Shield Forces in the Saudi/STC dispute; raised it for the anti-Houthi integration body.
- Syria reporting showed continued ISIL operations; balanced Syria Aleppo target between SDF and Islamic State/ISIL.
- Corrected the March crashed-plane question after rereading background: it concerns South America, not MENA.
- Raised ICE on federal-agency questions after new Maryland ICE shooting coverage.

## 2025-12-26 Update

No resolution events in `../state.json`; all 331 questions remained unresolved with active predictions.

Updates made:
- Somalia/Somaliland: Al Jazeera and Straits Times reported Israel formally recognised Somaliland and Somalia convened urgent discussions/denounced the move. Raised Israel sharply for the question about Somalia's minister saying a foreign country supported separatist regions; also raised Israel for the severed-agreements question, while keeping Ethiopia as a meaningful backup because Somalia-Israel formal agreements may be limited.
- Manchester United: 26 Dec Sky Sports coverage still framed United as Ruben Amorim's side, with no evidence Michael Carrick has taken over. Switched the Manchester derby manager forecast to Amorim.
- NFL: Sky Sports reported both Detroit and Kansas City eliminated from playoff contention. Removed Lions/Chiefs exposure from the Super Bowl forecast, raised Denver/Seattle/49ers, and replaced Jared Goff in the NFC champion QB market with Sam Darnold/Brock Purdy/Matthew Stafford/Caleb Williams alongside Jalen Hurts.

## 2025-12-27 Update

No resolution events in `../state.json`; all 331 questions remained unresolved with active predictions.

Updates made:
- Somalia/Somaliland: Al Jazeera directly quoted Somalia's State Minister for Foreign Affairs Ali Omar calling Israel's Somaliland recognition state aggression/interference. Raised Israel to near-certain for the minister/support-to-separatists question. Did not materially raise the severed-agreements question because the reporting still did not say Somalia had severed any agreements.
- Libya: Al Jazeera reported that Libya's army chief Mohammed Ali Ahmed al-Haddad was killed with other officers in a private jet crash near Ankara, and Turkiye held a military ceremony. Replaced the Saif al-Islam anchor on the Al Jazeera Arabic "killed in Libya" question with al-Haddad, keeping one transliteration variant.
- Manchester United: Sky Sports and Straits Times continued to identify Ruben Amorim as United boss after the Newcastle win, so raised Amorim further for the Jan. 17 derby.
- AFCON/Morocco: Morocco drew Mali; Brahim Diaz converted Morocco's penalty while Achraf Hakimi remained an unused substitute recovering from ankle injury. Added Brahim Diaz and trimmed Hakimi for the final stoppage-time missed-penalty question.

Watched but no update:
- Yemen/STC articles reinforce the Saudi-backed government vs STC standoff, but they did not identify the anti-Houthi integration body more clearly than the current National Shield Forces/PLC mix.
- Ukraine peace-talk articles confirm Zelenskyy/Trump talks in Florida, but not the January three-way talks city/mediator or Ukraine reshuffle names.
- Thailand-Cambodia ceasefire coverage named Thai officials but did not change the NIDA election-poll forecasts.

## 2025-12-28 Update

No resolution events in `../state.json`; all 331 questions remained unresolved with active predictions.

Updates made:
- Marc Guehi: Sky Sports/BBC paper talk said Guehi is expected to see out the final six months at Crystal Palace despite Liverpool/Man City links. Lowered Liverpool and added Man City/Crystal Palace.
- Bangladesh election: Reuters/Straits Times reported the National Citizen Party has allied with Jamaat-e-Islami while BNP is regaining momentum. Raised Jamaat for main opposition; kept NCP as a smaller backup.
- Libya: Al Jazeera's follow-up used the shorter name "General Mohammed al-Haddad" and reported burial in Misrata. Shifted the Libya killing question toward that shorter spelling while retaining full-name variants.
- AFCON/Morocco: Reuters/Straits Times reported Achraf Hakimi is fit for Zambia after missing the opening two Morocco games. Raised Hakimi back above Brahim Diaz for the final penalty question.
- Thailand: AP/Independent reported official PM candidate registration. People's Party's Natthaphong remains the main Anutin challenger; Sihasak is Bhumjaithai backup and Yodchanan Wongsawat is Pheu Thai's main candidate. Updated the NIDA PM poll forecast accordingly.
- AFCON semifinal: Algeria sealed a last-16 place with six points and no goals conceded; raised Algeria as Morocco's likely semifinal opponent.
- NFL: Packers lost again and Chicago clinched the NFC North; updated Super Bowl/NFC QB forecasts to include more Bears/Caleb Williams probability and remove the Eagles from the top-five Super Bowl set.
- Ukraine talks: Trump hosted Zelenskyy at Mar-a-Lago and said talks are in final stages, with a Putin call around the meeting. Added Palm Beach/United States as plausible settings/mediator signals for the January and February Russia-Ukraine talks questions, while keeping Turkey/Istanbul live.
- Bangladesh unrest identity: coverage of Sharif Osman Hadi's killing and Dipu Chandra Das's lynching supplied stronger name anchors for the future mass-protest/death question, though the "police custody" wording remains unmatched.

Watched but no update:
- Somalia-Israel language escalated further, but still no report that Somalia severed agreements.
- Niger ordered general mobilisation, but there was no move to dissolve all political parties.
- Yemen/STC and Thai-Cambodia coverage did not clarify their respective forecast targets beyond existing probabilities.
